items.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran says hotels housing U.S. soldiers in Gulf region will be targeted
items.summary: Kuwait's main commercial port was damaged in a drone attack on March 27; Lebanon’s official National News Agency reported that “enemy aircraft” carried out a raid on Tahouitet al-Ghadir in the southern suburbs at dawn

items.title: Donald Trump’s signature on dollar bills to mark 250th year of U.S. Independence
items.summary: The decision, taken by the U.S. Treasury, coincides with the 250th anniversary celebrations of American Independence this year

items.title: Balendra Shah takes oath as Nepal’s prime minister
items.summary: Nepal’s youngest prime minister has taken the oath of office on Friday after his party won a landslide victory in elections earlier this month following a youth-led uprising in September that toppled the government

items.title: Israel launches new wave of strikes on Iran with no sign of diplomatic breakthrough
items.summary: Israel’s attack on targets “in the heart of Tehran” came after the country said it would be “intensifying” its strikes on Iranian weapons production facilities

items.title: The Oscars are leaving Hollywood, to move to downtown Los Angeles
Epstein survivors sue US govt, Google over revealed identities
items.summary: “Survivors now face renewed trauma. Strangers call them, email them, threaten their physical safety, and accuse them of conspiring with Epstein when they are, in reality, Epstein's victims."

items.title: Five people injured in helicopter crash on Hawaiian island of Kauai
items.summary: The helicopter was carrying one pilot and four passengers when it crashed at Kalalau Beach, the Kauai Fire Department said

items.title: From beer to cosmetics, Asia feels full force of war-fuelled energy crisis
items.summary: Asia, which relies more heavily on crude ‌oil, gas, fuel and fertilizer from West Asia than other parts of the world, is the most vulnerable to supply disruption

items.title: U.S.-Israel-Iran war: List of countries allowed to cross the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Global oil and gas prices have surged after Iran virtually blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow shipping lane between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, that handles roughly 20 per cent of global oil and LNG (Liquefied Natural Gas).

items.title: Vance to visit Hungary days before Orbán’s election challenge, Foreign Minister says
items.summary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance is planning a visit to Hungary as Prime Minister Viktor Orbán fights a close election and courts support from Washington

items.title: Pornhub, Stripchat, XNXX, XVideos charged with breaching EU tech rules, risk fines
items.summary: “Children are ‌accessing adult content at increasingly younger ages and these platforms must put in place robust, privacy-preserving and effective measures ‌to keep minors off their services,” EU tech chief Henna ‌Virkkunen ⁠said

items.title: Watch: Multiple drones strike U.S. embassy in Baghdad
items.summary: The U.S. embassy in Baghdad came under attack early Tuesday, March 17, as drones targeted the heavily fortified compound in Iraq’s capital. Eyewitness footage showed air defence systems firing into the night sky, intercepting incoming drones during what Iraqi security sources described as one of the most intense assaults in recent weeks.

items.title: Bangladesh envoy calls for amicable resolution of 'sensitive' issues between New Delhi, Dhaka
items.summary: Mr. Hamidullah reaffirmed Dhaka's commitment to a "mutually beneficial" partnership, emphasizing the need to chart a pathway toward a deeper relationship that transcends existing "differences or divergences"

items.title: Ballots without a country: a quiet case for pluralism in Myanmar
items.summary: In a country where geography mirrors diversity and authority remains contested, elections in Myanmar unfold detached from realities on the ground; with key Opposition forces excluded and large swathes of the nation outside effective participation, the exercise underscores how the absence of pluralism and federal accommodation continue to undermine any meaningful democratic transition

items.title: China's top chipmaker has supplied chipmaking tech to Iran military, U.S. officials say
items.summary: SMIC, which was added to a trade blacklist in 2020 that restricts its access to U.S. exports, has denied allegations that it ‌has ties to the Chinese military-industrial complex

items.title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz to April 6
items.summary: Trump made the statement on March 26, in a social media post; he said he will hold off on bombing Iran's energy plants

items.title: The recipe behind China’s EV boom: Technology over price
items.summary: XPeng founder He Xiaopeng says intelligent driving and in-house innovation are powering China’s EV boom, even as competition intensifies, tech firms enter the space, and new bets on robotics and flying cars reshape mobility

items.title: Iran-Israel war highlights: Trump delays Strait of Hormuz deadline as Wall Street has biggest loss of war
items.summary: A day after Tehran dismissed Trump’s 15-point ceasefire plan, the President said Iran was “begging to make a deal” and urged its leaders to “get serious soon” about negotiations

items.title: Two Mexican humanitarian aid boats en-route to Cuba missing
items.summary: The Navy said ‌the two boats left Isla Mujeres, in the Mexican Caribbean state ‌of Quintana Roo, on March 20 bound ‌for ⁠Havana with nine crew members ⁠of different nationalities on board

items.title: U.S. judge blocks Pentagon's Anthropic blacklisting for now
items.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth’s unprecedented move, ​which followed Anthropic’s refusal to allow the ‌military to use AI chatbot Claude for U.S. surveillance or autonomous weapons, blocked Anthropic from certain military contracts.

items.title: Trump suggests that Iran let ten oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Trump made ⁠the comments at a Cabinet meeting in the ​White House

items.title: Jaishankar, French counterpart discuss Iran, Hormuz at G7 meet
items.summary: The participation of External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar in the G7 meetings signified “the importance France attaches to closely associating India — currently holding the BRICS presidency — with its G7 presidency”, the French government said

items.title: Trump threatens Iran to make a deal or the U.S. will “keep blowing them away”
items.summary: Donald Trump issues a stark warning to Iran, saying the U.S. will “keep blowing them away” if a deal is not reached. The statement comes amid rising tensions in West Asia, fuelling fears of further escalation.

items.title: India, other friendly countries granted passage through Hormuz: Iran
items.summary: Ships of India, China, Russia, Iraq, and Pak. can pass through the Strait, says Iran Foreign Minister, who thanks India, Sri Lanka for ‘significant help’; ‘sovereignty’ established over waterway, he adds

items.title: Panel wants prosecution of ousted Nepal PM Oli over violence in Gen Z protests
items.summary: If prosecuted and found guilty by a court, Oli, Lekhak and Khapung could face up to 10 years in jail

items.title: Indian among two killed in UAE by debris of intercepted missiles
items.summary: An Indian and a Pakistani were killed in Abu Dhabi by missile debris; the Indian Embassy offers condolences and support

items.title: Russian court bans Oscar-winning documentary ‘Mr. Nobody Against Putin’
items.summary: Russia’s Kremlin-appointed human rights council complained about the film last week, saying that “images of minors were used without obtaining the consent of their parents”

items.title: Fuel crisis updates: PM Modi to interact with CMs; review preparedness, plans on West Asia conflict
items.summary:
India has 60 days of oil stocks, one month of LPG supplies, says Government; 
Nayara Energy raised petrol prices by ₹5 per litre and diesel by ₹3 a litre

items.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes kill five as Hezbollah claims new attacks
items.summary: Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem said any negotiations with Israel under fire would amount to “surrender”, as he called on Lebanon’s authorities to rethink their decision to “criminalise” his group’s military activities

items.title: China warns U.S. against building arms facility in Philippines
items.summary: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said that Beijing would “firmly safeguard its territorial sovereignty and security interests”

items.title: Iran’s Parliament working on bill to impose fees on ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Imposing fees would end that and likely be strongly opposed by the Gulf Arab states, the United States and others

items.title: Iran-Israel war: A day‑by‑day rundown of the escalating crisis
items.summary: A joint U.S.-Israel attack on Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei have plunged West Asia into a fast‑spreading war, with Tehran and its allies launching retaliatory strikes across Israel, the Gulf and U.S. bases.

items.title: Pakistan says 'U.S.-Iran indirect talks are taking place'
items.summary: The United States has shared 15 points, being deliberated upon by Iran. Brotherly countries of Turkiye and Egypt, among others, are also extending their support to this initiative, says Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar

items.title: U.S. appeals court sides with Trump administration on detaining immigrants without bond
items.summary: Historically, bond was often granted to those without criminal convictions who were not flight risks, and mandatory detention was limited to recent border crossers.

items.title: North Korea, Belarus sign ‘friendship’ treaty during Lukashenko visit
items.summary: Besides supporting Russia’s war against Ukraine — around 2,000 North Korean soldiers are thought to have died — both nations are under Western sanctions and are accused of gross human rights violations

items.title: Israel Defence Minister claims Iran Guards navy commander killed in strike
items.summary: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said Alireza Tangsiri had been killed along with other senior naval commanders in a strike overnight; Iran did not immediately acknowledge Tangsiri's killing

items.title: Chinese Envoy assures Pakistan of 'continued support' in meeting with PM Shehbaz Sharif
items.summary: Ambassador Jiang “commended Pakistan’s economic resilience and reform efforts, and reaffirmed China’s continued support, particularly in trade and investment”

items.title: Israel took Iran's Araghchi, Qalibaf off hit list on Pakistan request: report
items.summary: Iran is reviewing a 15-point proposal from U.S. President Donald Trump, sent through Pakistan, to end the war

items.title: Iran War tests European unity amid German economic slowdown and fears over Ukraine support
items.summary: The stand of EU member states in the conflict is divergent as Europe faces economic slowdown and inflation amid rising oil and gas prices; Germany fears opposing U.S.-Israel actions could prompt U.S. to withdraw support from Ukraine, while the war could hurt its already faltering economy more

items.title: Iran, U.S. harden their positions as Tehran keeps its grip on Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Tehran moved to formalise its control over the crucial Strait of Hormuz while Washington prepared for the arrival of U.S. combat forces in the region that could be used on the ground

items.title: Trump to name Zuckerberg, Ellison, Huang to tech panel: Report
items.summary: The ‌President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST) could ultimately include 24 people, according to an executive order

items.title: G7 allies meet in France to narrow transatlantic Iran split
items.summary: British Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper on March 24, voiced unease that the war on Iran had shifted focus away from the Gaza peace plan and violence in the occupied West Bank

items.title: Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman calls March 25, 1971 genocide as ‘pre-planned massacre’
items.summary: “The Great Independence Day inspires us anew with the spirit of courage, sacrifice and patriotism. The fundamental goal of independence was to establish a Bangladesh that is free from discrimination, and to be democratic, peaceful and prosperous,” Tarique Rahman said

items.title: Trump to visit China in May after rescheduling trip due to Iran war
items.summary: Asked if the war would conclude by the time of the meeting in May, the White House Press Secretary said, “Again, as I've said, we've always estimated approximately four to six weeks.”

items.title: Samsung, SK urge employees to cut car use as energy risks rise over Iran crisis
items.summary: Samsung Electronics ‌and SK Group ​said they were ⁠asking employees to curb private car ‌use and follow fuel-saving measures

items.title: Earthquake of magnitude 4 hits northwestern Nepal
items.summary: There were no immediate reports of any damage caused by it

items.title: German army eyes AI tools to expedite wartime decision-making
items.summary: Lieutenant General Christian Freuding, ‌who became army chief last October after years of overseeing Germany’s arms supplies to Kyiv, shared his ‌insights from visits to Ukrainian command posts

items.title: Jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial
items.summary: Large technology ​companies in the U.S. have faced mounting criticism in the last decade over child and teen safety

items.title: At least 24 killed as bus plunges into Padma river in Bangladesh; search on for missing
items.summary: According to Rajbari district administration officials, Prime Minister Tarique Rahman called them for updates on the salvage campaign and ordered an investigation into the accident

items.title: Iran says it is reviewing a U.S. ceasefire plan but no talks
items.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi ​said while there had been no dialogue or negotiation with the U.S., various messages had been exchanged through intermediaries

items.title: Watch: Iran wants to make a deal ‘so badly, but they’re afraid to say it’: Trump
Iran-Israel war highlights: Israel strikes Tehran as Trump says U.S. negotiating to end war
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said it had launched a new wave of attacks against ‌locations in Israel including Tel Aviv and Kiryat Shmona, as well as U.S. bases in Kuwait, Jordan ‌and Bahrain

items.title: Trump says Iran ‘afraid’ to admit it wants a deal
items.summary: Donald Trump’s comments came after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said that “we do not intend to negotiate”

items.title: Pulitzer-winning author Tracy Kidder, who turned unlikely subjects into bestsellers, dies at 80
items.summary: Kidder's longtime publisher Random House confirmed his death in a statement on March 25

items.title: ‘Someone asked if my book was influenced by Dhurandhar’: author Sarnath Banerjee
items.summary: Banerjee’s new book, Absolute Jafar, is an India-Pakistan love story that unfolds across Delhi, Chicago, Karachi and Berlin

items.title: U.S. to deploy 1,000 elite troops to West Asia
items.summary: In a latest addition to the Iran war effort, U.S. officials say thousands of Marines, as well as rapid response naval vessels, will be heading to the region; Iran and Israel exchange strikes; Gulf neighbours Kuwait and Saudi Arabia come under fire; Iran’s military says it also targeted a U.S. aircraft carrier

items.title: Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow, Kyiv escalate aerial barrages
items.summary: Alexander Drozdenko, Governor of the Leningrad region north of Moscow, said 56 drones were shot down there, and a fire broke out in the Baltic Sea port of Ust-Luga as the result of Ukraine’s attack

items.title: Kim vows North Korea's 'unshakable' support for Russia
items.summary: Analysts say the assistance has been provided in exchange for Russia’s provision of food and weapons technology

items.title: Rights group warns of 'dystopian' Hong Kong after bookstore arrests
items.summary: “First, the authorities jailed the newspaper publisher, then they arrested the person selling books about him. Who’s next?” Elaine Pearson, HRW’s Asia director, said

items.title: What is at stake at the WTO’s MC14? | Explained
items.summary: What is the context in which MC14 is taking place? What has weakened the WTO’s dispute settlement system? What are the key issues before MC14? Why is the e-commerce moratorium contentious? What role should India play at MC14?
